title: clerodendrum haematolasium
intro: Clerodendrum haematolasium, also known as the bleeding heart tree, is a tropical plant native to the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Clerodendrum haematolasium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
